qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: STOMP pre-authentication integer overflow leads to uncontrolled memory allocation and denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a missing authentication vulnerability (CWE-306) in the Core protocol federation mechanism allowing unauthenticated rogue broker connections for message injection/exfiltration. The finding describes a STOMP protocol integer overflow in StompDecoder.parseBody() causing denial of service. These differ in protocol (Core vs STOMP), root cause (missing auth check for federation vs integer overflow in content-length parsing), vulnerable code (federation/connection handling vs StompDecoder.java:216-219), and impact (message injection/exfiltration vs broker crash DoS).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-fw88-pf9m-p947/CVE-2026-27446) describes a missing authentication flaw (CWE-306) in the Apache Artemis Core protocol that allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to force a target broker to establish an outbound Core federation connection to an attacker-controlled rogue broker, enabling message injection into and exfiltration from any queue. The sole qualifying finding describes a pre-authentication integer overflow in the STOMP protocol's StompDecoder.parseBody() that causes denial of service via OutOfMemoryError. These are fundamentally different vulnerabilities: different protocols (Core vs STOMP), different root causes (missing authorization check on federation vs integer overflow in content-length header parsing), different vulnerable code paths (Core federation/connection handling vs StompDecoder.java lines 216-219), and different security impacts (confidentiality/integrity via message injection/exfiltration vs availability via DoS). The two non-qualifying findings (OpenWire ObjectMessage deserialization, XXE via XPath selector) are also unrelated to the advisory-described vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory describes CWE-306 Missing Authentication for Critical Function in Core protocol federation
Advisory impact: message injection/exfiltration via rogue broker federation connections
Only qualifying finding is STOMP integer overflow DoS (StompDecoder.java:216-219)
Different protocol: advisory targets Core protocol, finding targets STOMP protocol
Different root cause: advisory is missing auth check, finding is integer overflow in content-length parsing
Different impact: advisory enables message injection/exfiltration (C/I), finding causes DoS (A only)
Non-qualifying findings (OpenWire deserialization, XXE) also do not match the advisory
